questions.questionText: The best way to validate that a database backup is working, is to:
options: A. Periodically recover from the backup file
B. Check the backup logs every day
C. Appoint a special DBA in charge of backups
D. Check the size of the backup file
E. Check for the automatic email notification of backup success
questions.correctAnswer: A. Periodically recover from the backup file

questions.questionText: When reviewing data access plans, sequential searching is slowing the database.One way to fix this is:
options: A. Reducing the number of database users
B. Creating new indexes
C. Converting it to an in-memory database
D. Moving the database to the cloud
E. Adding more memory
questions.correctAnswer: B. Creating new indexes

questions.questionText: What area do you not consider when developing a 'data governance operating model'?
options: A. The availability of industry data models
B. The business model â€“ decentralized versus centralized
C. The value of data to the organisation
D. Cultural factors - such as acceptance of discipline and adaptability to change
E. Impact of regulation
questions.correctAnswer: A. The availability of industry data models

questions.questionText: The purpose of data governance is to ensure that:
options: A. Data can be reported on by the business line
B. Data is backed up every night
C. Data will be understood by all stakeholders
D. Data is available for other systems to use
E. Data is managed properly, according to policies and best practices
questions.correctAnswer: E. Data is managed properly, according to policies and best practices

questions.questionText: A common driver for data governance is:
options: A. Irreconcilable figures in reports
B. Regulatory compliance
C. The appointment of a CDO
D. Internal audits
E. Outsourcing
questions.correctAnswer: B. Regulatory compliance

questions.questionText: Adoption of a Data Governance program is most likely to succeed:
options: A. When dictated by senior executives
B. When the entire enterprise is partaking at once
C. In 1 or 2Â months with a large consulting team
D. When the CDO is a charismatic leader
E. With an incremental rollout strategy
questions.correctAnswer: E. With an incremental rollout strategy

questions.questionText: In 2009, ARMA International published GARP for managing records and information.GARP stands for:
options: A. Generally Accepted Recordkeeping Principles
B. Generally Available Recordkeeping Practices
C. Gregarious Archive of Recordkeeping Processes
D. Global Accredited Recordkeeping Principles
E. G20 Approved Recordkeeping Principles
questions.correctAnswer: A. Generally Accepted Recordkeeping Principles

questions.questionText: 'Planning, implementation and control activities for lifecycle management of data and information, found in any form or medium', pertains to which knowledge area?
options: A. Data Warehousing and Business Intelligence
B. Data Integration and Interoperability
C. Metadata Management
D. Document and Content Management
E. Data Storage and Operations
questions.correctAnswer: D. Document and Content Management

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a reason why organisations do not dispose of non-value-adding information?
options: A. The organisation's data quality benchmark diminishes
B. Data modelling the content is hard to reproduce
C. The information is never out of date
D. Storage is cheap and easily expanded
E. The metadata repository cannot be updated
questions.correctAnswer: D. Storage is cheap and easily expanded

questions.questionText: An enterprise content management system (ECM) stores the following:
options: A. Kanban boards
B. Backups of the enterprise's data warehouse
C. Code and test scripts for enterprise development environments
D. Documents and images, but not multi-media
E. Information to be displayed on websites and contained in documents
questions.correctAnswer: E. Information to be displayed on websites and contained in documents

questions.questionText: Which of the following activities is NOT a way that enterprise data architecture influences the scope boundaries of projects?
options: A. Ensuring enterprise business processes are effectively documented
B. Performing design reviews to ensure support of long-term organisational strategy
C. Providing enterprise data requirement for projects
D. Enforcing data architecture standards
E. Ensuring sufficient data replication controls are in place
questions.correctAnswer: A. Ensuring enterprise business processes are effectively documented

questions.questionText: Data flows can be represented by:
options: A. Two dimensional matrices showing the relationships between data users and business processes
B. Two dimensional matrices showing the relationships between data entities and business processes
C. Two dimensional matrices showing the relationships between data facts and data dimensions
D. Two dimensional matrices showing the relationships between data entities and data quality
E. Two dimensional matrices showing the relationships between data users and data consumers
questions.correctAnswer: B. Two dimensional matrices showing the relationships between data entities and business processes

questions.questionText: The key architecture domains include:
options: A. Business, strategy, application and technology architectures
B. Zachmann, TOGAF, Cobit, and Heath architectures
C. Business, data, infrastructure and technology architectures
D. Business, data, application and technology architectures
E. Process, database, software and technology architectures
questions.correctAnswer: D. Business, data, application and technology architectures

questions.questionText: An enterprise data model would be composed of:
options: A. Conceptual models, logical models and physical models
B. Conceptual models, star schema models and interface models
C. Conceptual models, subject area models and logical models
D. Logical models, physical models and infrastructure models
E. Enterprise models, data models and compositional models
questions.correctAnswer: C. Conceptual models, subject area models and logical models

questions.questionText: A relationship that allows an address to be used by multiple people, and each person can have multiple addresses, can be resolved:
options: A. With an additional relationship describing the address usage
B. By changing the primary keys on Person and Address to ensure referential integrity
C. With an associative entity called Person Address Usage and two, 'one to many' relationships
D. With a partnership entity called Person Address Usage and two, 'one to many' relationships
E. By changing the role names of the foreign keys on Person and Address to ensure referential integrity
questions.correctAnswer: C. With an associative entity called Person Address Usage and two, 'one to many' relationships

questions.questionText: In a data model, domains can be used for:
options: A. Reducing the amount of coding required in the database
B. Public viewing of the data model
C. Consolidation of data into subject areas
D. Consistency and standardization of data values
E. Separating concepts into business ideas
questions.correctAnswer: D. Consistency and standardization of data values

questions.questionText: Data that is required across business processes, areas and systems is called:
options: A. Event Data
B. A Data Mart
C. Static Data
D. Reference and Master Data
E. Important Data
questions.correctAnswer: D. Reference and Master Data

questions.questionText: The difference between a System of Record and a System of Reference is:
options: A. They are the same thing
B. A system of record is a trusted way to access the data; a system of reference is the original source of the data
C. A system of record is the source of master data; a system of reference is the source of reference data
D. A system of record is the original source of the data; the system of reference is a trusted way to access the data
E. A system of reference is the source of transaction data; the system of record is the source of master data
questions.correctAnswer: D. A system of record is the original source of the data; the system of reference is a trusted way to access the data

questions.questionText: A goal of reference and master data management is for data to ensure shared data is:
options: A. Secure, auditable, complete and relevant
B. Secure, auditable, publicly available and free
C. Continuous, consistent, current and private
D. Complete, consistent, current and authoritative
E. Complete, consistent, content and relevant
questions.correctAnswer: D. Complete, consistent, current and authoritative

questions.questionText: An authoritative system where data is created/captured, and/or maintained through a defined set of rules and expectations is called:
options: A. A System of Referential Integrity
B. A System of Retirement
C. A System of Reference
D. A System of Systems
E. A System of Record
questions.correctAnswer: E. A System of Record

questions.questionText: Data Management pays more attention to the:
options: A. The underlying problems in data than the root causes
B. Use of data rather than the provision of data
C. Provision of data rather than the usage of data
D. Visualisation of data rather than the narrative surrounding data
E. Data than information and knowledge
questions.correctAnswer: C. Provision of data rather than the usage of data

questions.questionText: In the common enterprise architecture model coded BIAT, the 'I' stands for:
options: A. Interoperability
B. Instance
C. Identification
D. Integration
E. Information
questions.correctAnswer: E. Information

questions.questionText: One of the ongoing issues in maintaining ALL the customer data in a single master data management data store is that:
options: A. The need for differing customer security levels across the enterprise will be entirely avoided
B. There will be continuous changes to the solution as it is sensitive to any change in any other customer system
C. Data warehousing will find it difficult to pick a system of reference or record for loading of data
D. The project will be very expensive
E. There will be isolated reference data left behind, making it difficult to manage
questions.correctAnswer: B. There will be continuous changes to the solution as it is sensitive to any change in any other customer system

questions.questionText: By comparing the system of record and systems of reference to each other, it is possible to:
options: A. Validate the accuracy of the master data
B. Update the core reference values
C. Construct time variant sequences
D. Validate the completeness of the master data
E. Validate the consistency of the master data
questions.correctAnswer: E. Validate the consistency of the master data

questions.questionText: The search function associated with a document management store is failing to return known artefacts. This is due to a failure of:
options: A. Data privacy and confidentiality procedures
B. Maintaining public access to all documents in the document management store
C. Business intelligence implementation
D. Maintaining appropriate metadata on each document
E. Effective data quality metrics
questions.correctAnswer: D. Maintaining appropriate metadata on each document

questions.questionText: A report displaying birth date contains possible, but incorrect values. What is a possible explanation?
options: A. Birth date is populated from a single source system, which contains missing values
B. Birth date is populated from two source systems, both of which record the birth date in the birth date field
C. Birth date is populated from a single source system, which does not contain birth date
D. Birth date is populated from two source systems, one of which stores marriage date in the birth date field
E. Birth date is populated from a single source system, where the date field is an offset value of 1601
questions.correctAnswer: D. Birth date is populated from two source systems, one of which stores marriage date in the birth date field

questions.questionText: IoT devices have introduced new vulnerabilities by:
options: A. Using standard network connections without the sophisticated security provided by more powerful network devices
B. Using standard network connections and overwhelming the network with the volume of data
C. Using non-standard network connections which means that less attackers understand the technology
D. Using non-standard network connections which are not managed to the same level of quality
E. Using global network connection
questions.correctAnswer: A. Using standard network connections without the sophisticated security provided by more powerful network devices

questions.questionText: A DMZ is bordered by 2 firewalls. These are between the DMZ and the:
options: A. Internet, for added security
B. Internet and intranet
C. Internet and internal systems
D. Korean peninsula
E. Internet and extranet
questions.correctAnswer: B. Internet and intranet

questions.questionText: Over a decade an organisation has rationalised implementation of party concepts from 48Â systems to 3. This is a result of good:
options: A. Data quality and data governance
B. Data operations and system rationalization
C. Data architecture and data warehousing
D. Data architecture and data governance
E. System rationalization and metadata management
questions.correctAnswer: D. Data architecture and data governance

questions.questionText: A data architect is best deployed:
options: A. To build the database solution
B. To manage the delivery of all the data aspects of a program
C. After the project completes to identify weaknesses and lessons learned
D. During the early stages of a project to define and shape a strategic solution
E. By project managers to sign off all data deliverables
questions.correctAnswer: D. During the early stages of a project to define and shape a strategic solution

questions.questionText: When trying to integrate a large number of systems, the integration complexities can be reduced by:
options: A. The use of a common data model
B. Using data quality measures and targets
C. Clear business specification and priorities
D. The use of SQL
E. Tackling the largest systems first
questions.correctAnswer: A. The use of a common data model

questions.questionText: In order to gain endorsement to extend the use of reference and master data across the enterprise, a key supporting DMBOK knowledge area is:
options: A. Data Architecture
B. Data Governance
C. Data Storage and Operations
D. Data Security
E. Document and Content Management
questions.correctAnswer: B. Data Governance

questions.questionText: The creation of overly complex enterprise integration over time is often a symptom of:
options: A. Multiple data owners
B. Multiple integration technologies
C. Multiple data warehouses
D. Multiple metadata tags
E. Multiple application coding languages
questions.correctAnswer: B. Multiple integration technologies

questions.questionText: A 'Content Distribution Network' supporting a multi-national website is likely to use:
options: A. A database backup and restore solution
B. An extract transform and load solution
C. A records disposal solution
D. A replication solution
E. An archiving solution
questions.correctAnswer: D. A replication solution

questions.questionText: First Normal Form:
options: A. Ensures that each attribute has a balanced proportion of neutrons and protons
B. Ensures that each entity has no hidden primary keys
C. Ensures that the Party Role structure is correctly represented
D. Ensures that each attribute is atomic without repeating groups
E. Ensures that each attribute has a definition that makes sense
questions.correctAnswer: D. Ensures that each attribute is atomic without repeating groups

questions.questionText: In data modelling practice, entities are linked by:
options: A. Triggers
B. Cardinality
C. Processes
D. Indexes
E. Relationships
questions.correctAnswer: E. Relationships

questions.questionText: Data Models are critical to effective management of data, because they:
options: A. Control the list of values in dropdown lists in applications
B. Strategically prepare organisations to quickly evolve their products and services
C. Define the rules and approval mechanisms to make changes to the data structures
D. Determine which style of data schema is used in a data warehouse
E. Provide a common vocabulary around data
questions.correctAnswer: E. Provide a common vocabulary around data

questions.questionText: The goal of data modelling, documenting different perspectives and aligning them in a single data model, achieves:
options: A. Applications that are deployed by simple version upgrades
B. Application projects that are less complicated due to a variant data model
C. Application performance that is guaranteed
D. Applications that meet the custom needs of specific business areas
E. Applications that more closely align to current and future business requirements
questions.correctAnswer: E. Applications that more closely align to current and future business requirements

questions.questionText: Significant operational issues have been caused by the implementation of a data model that represents a Customer Agreement as a ternary relationship between the Enterprise, a Customer, and their Contact Person. Which of the following describes the likely operational issue?
options: A. Every time the customer changes addresses the address for the contact person must change as well
B. In the event of a merger between enterprises, the contact person addresses need to be updated
C. Every time the contact person changes, the customer agreement needs to be re-established
D. Response time for retrieving the Customer Agreement degrades rapidly due to the lack of indexing
E. Every time the Customer Agreement was renewed, a new Contact Person record was required
questions.correctAnswer: C. Every time the contact person changes, the customer agreement needs to be re-established

questions.questionText: A project scope includes the collection, exchange and reporting of data from multiple systems. Conceptual, logical and physical data models are maintained. How many models of each type can be expected?
options: A. More logical data models than physical data models, and more logical data models than conceptual data models
B. The same number of each of the model types
C. More conceptual data models than logical data models, and more logical data models than physical data models
D. Only 1 conceptual data model, 1 logical data model and 1 physical data model
E. More physical data models than logical data models, and more logical data models than conceptual data models
questions.correctAnswer: E. More physical data models than logical data models, and more logical data models than conceptual data models

questions.questionText: A workforce is locked out of a building due to a threat to personnel. The Business Continuity plan (BCP) is stored in the Document Management System (DMS).Luckily:
options: A. The security policy includes such a scenario
B. The workforce are expendable
C. The document is printed and stored off site
D. The metadata repository holds the BCP
E. The data warehouse catalog is fully operational
questions.correctAnswer: C. The document is printed and stored off site

questions.questionText: Integrating data security with document and content management knowledge areas, guides the implementation of:
options: A. Fitness for purpose metrics for unstructured data
B. Straight-through processing for NoSQL queries
C. Appropriate access and authorization to structured data
D. Appropriate access and authorization to unstructured data
E. Appropriate privacy controls on data marts
questions.correctAnswer: D. Appropriate access and authorization to unstructured data

questions.questionText: Common data loss scenarios, that a DBA plans for, are unlikely to include:
options: A. Customers losing their user guide
B. Loss of database server
C. Corruption of the physical database
D. Loss of transaction logs
E. Loss of one or more disk storage devices
questions.correctAnswer: A. Customers losing their user guide

questions.questionText: Database monitoring tools measure key database metrics, such as:
options: A. Capacity, availability, cache performance, user statistics
B. Capacity, availability, backup instances, data quality
C. Create, read, normalization, user access
D. Create, read, update, delete
E. Capacity, design, normalization, user access
questions.correctAnswer: A. Capacity, availability, cache performance, user statistics

questions.questionText: The goals of data storage and operations are:
options: A. Managing accessibility, ensuring authorized access and managing performance
B. Managing availability, ensuring consistency and managing glossaries
C. Managing user experience, ensuring consistency and managing glossaries
D. Managing user experience, ensuring data access and managing data lifecycles
E. Managing availability, ensuring integrity and managing performance
questions.correctAnswer: E. Managing availability, ensuring integrity and managing performance

questions.questionText: Database performance depends upon two independent facets. These are:
options: A. Availability and speed
B. Choice of DBMS and programming language
C. Hardware and network
D. Distance to data center and network bandwidth
E. Number of users and number of tables
questions.correctAnswer: A. Availability and speed

questions.questionText: Achieving security risk reduction in an organisation begins with developing what?
options: A. A classification model, classifying each data concept and locating the physical data
B. A metadata model, locating the data and moving it into the metadata repository
C. A security model, classifying each organisational role and putting the physical data behind a firewall
D. A change management model, prioritising security changes and then updating the active directory
E. An enterprise data model, rolling out data flow diagrams and embedding security into the database
questions.correctAnswer: A. A classification model, classifying each data concept and locating the physical data

questions.questionText: A weak point in an organisationâ€™s defences is a:
options: A. Anti-virus software
B. Vulnerability
C. Security model problem
D. Firewall
E. Penetration
questions.correctAnswer: B. Vulnerability

questions.questionText: Primary drivers of data security activities are:
options: A. Risk control and content management
B. Risk reduction and business growth
C. Glossary management and risk reduction
D. Data protection and flexible database design
E. Data quality and intellectual property protection
questions.correctAnswer: B. Risk reduction and business growth

questions.questionText: The percentage of enterprise computers having the most recent security patch installed is a metric of which knowledge area?
options: A. Data Security
B. Data Storage and Operations
C. Metadata Management
D. Data Quality
E. Data Warehousing and Business Intelligence
questions.correctAnswer: A. Data Security

questions.questionText: A security mechanism that searches for customer bank account details in outgoing emails is achieving the goal of:
options: A. Ensuring stakeholder requirements for openness and transparency are met
B. Ensuring stakeholder requirements for service design and experience are met
C. Ensuring stakeholder requirements for response time and availability levels are met
D. Ensuring stakeholder requirements for concise definitions and usage are met
E. Ensuring stakeholder requirements for confidentiality and privacy are met
questions.correctAnswer: E. Ensuring stakeholder requirements for confidentiality and privacy are met

questions.questionText: Is the risk of a data breach higher in documents on a network drive or in an enterprise resource system (ERP)?
options: A. Data needs to be emailed to be breached effectively
B. The probability is exactly the same
C. Data cannot be breached from either platform due to the superior security in modern computer networks
D. On a network drive
E. In an enterprise resource system (ERP)
questions.correctAnswer: D. On a network drive

questions.questionText: Data Stewards are most likely to be responsible for:
options: A. Data Governance and Data Quality
B. Content and Document Management
C. Data Integration and Interoperability
D. Data Modelling and Data Security
E. Data Storage and Operations
questions.correctAnswer: A. Data Governance and Data Quality

questions.questionText: A 'Data Governance Strategy' usually includes the following deliverables:
options: A. A Charter, a Security Strategy, a Solution Roadmap, and a User Experience Plan
B. A Charter, a Data Architecture, a Program Data Model, and a Plan for Operational Success
C. A Charter, an Operating Framework, an Implementation Roadmap, and a Plan for Operational Success
D. A Statement of Intent, a Decision Framework, an Implementation Roadmap, and a Governance Toolkit
E. A Program Plan, a Resource Plan, an Implementation Plan, and a Test Plan
questions.correctAnswer: C. A Charter, an Operating Framework, an Implementation Roadmap, and a Plan for Operational Success

questions.questionText: Which of the following is the biggest challenge to establishing effective Data Governance?
options: A. Procuring large enough office space
B. Negotiating with the other C-suite holders
C. The amount of metadata to be loaded into a repository
D. The quantity of stakeholder change management required
E. The number and complexity of policies and procedures
questions.correctAnswer: D. The quantity of stakeholder change management required

questions.questionText: The advantage of a decentralized data governance model over a centralized model is:
options: A. An increased level of ownership from local decision making groups
B. The cheaper execution of data governance operations
C. The easier implementation of industry data models
D. Having a common approach to resolving data governance issues
E. The common metadata repository configurations
questions.correctAnswer: A. An increased level of ownership from local decision making groups

questions.questionText: The ability of an organisation to respond to changes in product configuration is easier due to generalisation in the:
options: A. Data warehousing
B. Technical architecture
C. Data quality
D. Business architecture
E. Data architecture
questions.correctAnswer: E. Data architecture

questions.questionText: A data architecture team is best described as:
options: A. The authors of reference data
B. A group of strong database administrators
C. A strategic planning and compliance team
D. A well-managed project of architectural development
E. An operational data provisioning group
questions.correctAnswer: C. A strategic planning and compliance team

questions.questionText: A key feature of the Bill Inmon's approach to data warehousing is:
options: A. A preference for supporting operational reporting
B. A normalized relational model to store and manage data
C. Its ability to operate on open source platforms
D. An exclusive focus on star schemas and cubes
E. A tight management of data dimensions
questions.correctAnswer: B. A normalized relational model to store and manage data

questions.questionText: The key concepts of the data vault are:
options: A. Hubs, links and satellites
B. Hubs, links and planets
C. Facts, links and satellites
D. Keys, locks and combinations
E. Facts, dimensions and time
questions.correctAnswer: A. Hubs, links and satellites

questions.questionText: In a data warehouse, where the classification lists for organisation type are inconsistent in different source systems, there is an indication that there is a lack of focus on:
options: A. Master Data
B. Reference data
C. Data Storage
D. Data Modelling
E. Metadata Management
questions.correctAnswer: B. Reference data

questions.questionText: The uncontrolled duplication of dimensions in a Kimball data warehouse is best controlled by:
options: A. Establishing approval processes after user testing and before implementation
B. Replacing the data modelling team
C. Adapting the data architecture techniques supporting an Inmon style
D. Increased reference data management in source systems
E. Establishing approval processes during requirements and design
questions.correctAnswer: E. Establishing approval processes during requirements and design

questions.questionText: A database uses foreign keys from code tables for column values. This is a way of implementing:
options: A. Temporal data
B. Event data
C. Star schema data
D. Master data
E. Reference data
questions.correctAnswer: E. Reference data

questions.questionText: An application uses a single service account for all database access. One of the risks of this approach is:
options: A. The ability to trace who made changes to the data
B. It constrains the application from running parallel processes
C. The data becomes out of order
D. The application freezes more often
E. The database runs out of threads
questions.correctAnswer: A. The ability to trace who made changes to the data

questions.questionText: Data that is used infrequently or not at all may be moved to an alternative data store.This is called:
options: A. Replication
B. Authentication
C. Analysis
D. Auditing
E. Archiving
questions.correctAnswer: E. Archiving

questions.questionText: Integrating two data stores using batch or real-time synchronous approaches results in a difference in:
options: A. Source of truth
B. Data quality
C. Timestamping
D. Lethargy
E. Latency
questions.correctAnswer: E. Latency

questions.questionText: Basic profiling of data involves analysis of:
options: A. Data sources, data visualisations, data latency and ownership
B. Data lakes, data warehouses and operational data stores
C. Data criteria, data acquisition, data policy and procedures inside data governance
D. Data formats, data values, patterns and relationships
E. Data clusters, data clouds, data repositories and metadata libraries
questions.correctAnswer: D. Data formats, data values, patterns and relationships

questions.questionText: Mapping requirements and rules for moving data from source to target enables:
options: A. Extract
B. Backups
C. Analysis
D. Load
E. Transformation
questions.correctAnswer: E. Transformation

questions.questionText: Big data is often defined by three characteristics. They are:
options: A. Volume, Variety and Velocity
B. Expansive, Engaged and Enormous
C. Direction, Depth and Details
D. Size, Speed and Sensitivity
E. Complexity, Compliance and Completeness
questions.correctAnswer: A. Volume, Variety and Velocity

questions.questionText: GDPR and PIPEDA are examples of:
options: A. Data protection regulations
B. Primary information parsing algorithms
C. Global data modelling standards
D. Data program rules
E. Content management systems
questions.correctAnswer: A. Data protection regulations

questions.questionText: Which DMBoK knowledge area is most likely responsible for a high percentage of returned mail?
options: A. Data Integration and Interoperability
B. Metadata Management
C. Reference and Master Data
D. Data Warehousing and Business Intelligence
E. Data Quality
questions.correctAnswer: E. Data Quality

questions.questionText: When a data quality team has more issues than they can manage, they should look to:
options: A. Initiate data quality improvement cycles, focusing on achieving incremental improvements
B. Hire more people
C. Establish a program of quick wins targeting easy fixes over a short time period
D. Delete any issue that is greater than 6Â months old
E. Implement data validation rules on data entry systems
questions.correctAnswer: A. Initiate data quality improvement cycles, focusing on achieving incremental improvements

questions.questionText: A complexity in documenting data lineage is:
options: A. Identifying source databases
B. Conflicting application requirements from data owners
C. Different data element names and formats
D. Choosing which content management software to use
E. Establishing data quality metrics
questions.correctAnswer: C. Different data element names and formats

questions.questionText: The Family Name of a Person is recorded in a system. The column name is Pname.Pname is an example of:
options: A. Metadata
B. Data
C. Megadata
D. Normalised data
E. Poor table design
questions.correctAnswer: E. Poor table design

questions.questionText: A data lineage tool enables a user to:
options: A. Visualize how the data gets to the data lake
B. Track the historical changes to a data value
C. Line up the data to support sophisticated glossary management
D. Enables rapid development of dashboard reporting
E. Track the data from source system to a target database; understanding its transformations
questions.correctAnswer: E. Track the data from source system to a target database; understanding its transformations

questions.questionText: Achieving near-real-time data replication, using a source accumulation technique, triggers on:
options: A. Request from a target system to pull data
B. Reaching a threshold or frequency schedule
C. Daily batch loads
D. Posting of messages on the enterprise bus
E. Arrival of streaming data
questions.correctAnswer: B. Reaching a threshold or frequency schedule

questions.questionText: During the implementation of a data warehouse, a roadmap is used to:
options: A. Articulate user requirements
B. Demonstrate progress towards the desired end state
C. Construct intricate security authorization
D. Demonstrate alignment to the project plan
E. Articulate data quality checkpoints
questions.correctAnswer: B. Demonstrate progress towards the desired end state

questions.questionText: One of the key differences between operational systems and data warehouses is:
options: A. Operational systems focus on business processes; data warehouses focus on business strategies
B. Operational systems focus on historical data; data warehouses contain current data
C. Operational systems are available 24x7; data warehouses are available during business hours
D. Operational systems focus on data quality; data warehouses focus on data security
E. Operational systems focus on current data; data warehouses contain historical data
questions.correctAnswer: E. Operational systems focus on current data; data warehouses contain historical data

questions.questionText: The 'Data Management Body of Knowledge' is produced by:
options: A. The Data Management Authority
B. The Data Management Association
C. The Project Management Institute
D. The Data Practitioner Association
E. The Data Analysis Association
questions.correctAnswer: B. The Data Management Association

questions.questionText: The DMBoK 'Environmental Factors hexagon' shows the relationship between:
options: A. People, process and technology
B. People, software and tools
C. Business, application and technology architecture
D. Inputs, activities and deliverables
E. DMBoK knowledge areas
questions.correctAnswer: A. People, process and technology

questions.questionText: Data and information are:
options: A. Representations of truth
B. Used only in the context of business intelligence
C. Intertwined and dependent on each other
D. Completely separate things
E. Pillars of the modern, organisational Parthenon
questions.correctAnswer: C. Intertwined and dependent on each other

questions.questionText: If data is a fact, why isnâ€™t it always easy to integrate?
options: A. The data elements are the same
B. The information context comes in different security formats

questions.correctAnswer: C. Obtained permission from an individual for the collection, use or disclosure of personal information

questions.questionText: A staff member has been detected inappropriately accessing client records from usage logs. The security mechanism being used is an:
options: A. Access
B. Authorisation
C. Audit
D. Authentication
E. Entitlement
questions.correctAnswer: C. Audit

questions.questionText: Which Data Architecture Artifact describes how data transforms into business assets?
options: A. Data Flows
B. Data Value Chain
C. Master Data Models
D. Implementation Roadmap
E. Business Value Chains
questions.correctAnswer: B. Data Value Chain

questions.questionText: Knowledge area activities are classified into four categories. They are:
options: A. Planning, Development, Control and Operations
B. Procurement, Development, Control and Operations
C. Procurement, Detailing, Control and Organizing
D. Planning, Detailing, Control and Organizing
E. Preparation, Detailing, Creating and Deployment
questions.correctAnswer: A. Planning, Development, Control and Operations

questions.questionText: According to the DMBoK, a business person who is accountable for decisions about data within their domain is called:
options: A. A Data Owner
B. An Enterprise Data Steward
C. A Business Data Steward
D. A Data Custodian
E. A Business Data Steward
questions.correctAnswer: A. A Data Owner

questions.questionText: The Data Governance Steering Committee is best described as:
options: A. The primary and highest authority responsible for the oversight and support of data governance activities
B. The local or divisional council, working under auspices of the CDO
C. The representatives of data use on project steering committees
D. The community of interest, focused on specific subject areas or projects
E. A burden to the agile delivery in a modern enterprise
questions.correctAnswer: A. The primary and highest authority responsible for the oversight and support of data governance activities

questions.questionText: In data integration, the goal of data discovery is to:
options: A. Assign data glossary terms and data formats
B. Identify key users and perform high level assessment of data quality
C. Identify potential sources and perform high-level assessment of data quality
D. Identify potential sources and assure data recovery processes are compliant
E. Assign data glossary terms and canonical models
questions.correctAnswer: C. Identify potential sources and perform high-level assessment of data quality

questions.questionText: Three common interaction models for data integration are:
options: A. Point to point, hub and spoke, publish and subscribe
B. Straight copy, curved copy, roundabout copy
C. Plane to point, harvest and seed, publish and subscribe
D. Point to point, wheel and spoke, public and share
E. Record and pass, copy and send, read and write
questions.correctAnswer: A. Point to point, hub and spoke, publish and subscribe

questions.questionText: Data modelling tools and model repositories are necessary for:
options: A. Enabling data governance
B. Visualizing and communicating database designs
C. Designing and visualizing linkages between metadata, reference data and transactional data repositories
D. Designing and visualizing organizational data artifacts
E. Managing the enterprise data model in all levels
questions.correctAnswer: E. Managing the enterprise data model in all levels

questions.questionText: A minimal super key is:
options: A. Also known as a candidate key, it is a superkey without duplicated attributes. I.e. any set of attributes without duplicates that uniquely identify an entity instance.
B. Any set of attributes where each attribute that makes up the key is a foreign key in its own right.
C. An artificial key, made up of several meaningful components to help the reader understand the nature of the entity from the key alone.
D. A synonym for a surrogate key.
E. A type of advanced index key structure, in the same family as Hash, Heap, B-Tree and Inverted.
questions.correctAnswer: A. Also known as a candidate key, it is a superkey without duplicated attributes. I.e. any set of attributes without duplicates that uniquely identify an entity instance.

questions.questionText: Provide a project related activity that a data architect performs when understanding business requirements:
options: A. Design data models that enforce business rules from a data lifecycle perspective
B. Identify data related pain points
C. Reuse Common Data Structures that support the business requirements
D. Before purchasing an application, they can reverse engineering the model to validate business requirements
E. Ensure that the scope is aligned with the enterprise data model
questions.correctAnswer: B. Identify data related pain points

questions.questionText: The conceptual Enterprise Data Model may be built using a top-down or bottom up approach. What is the difference?
options: A. Top-down starts with industry standard models. Bottom-up requires a subject area discriminator.
B. Top-down uses data flows as a starting point, while bottom-up starts with data lineage.
C. Bottom-up starts with subject areas and populating them with models. Top-down is based on existing data models.
D. Top-down starts with subject areas and populating them with models. Bottom-up is based on existing data models.
E. Top-down and bottom-up are two sides of the same coin.
questions.correctAnswer: D. Top-down starts with subject areas and populating them with models. Bottom-up is based on existing data models.

questions.questionText: How does the enterprise data model guide the project data specifications?
options: A. By verifying business requirements
B. By ensuring that Project data specifications deal with future requirements
C. By ensuring that the projects implement solutions across the entire data life cycle
D. By providing clear & consistent definitions across the organization
E. By providing the Application data models with structural blueprints
questions.correctAnswer: D. By providing clear & consistent definitions across the organization

questions.questionText: The loading of country codes into a CRM is a classic:
options: A. Reference data integration
B. Master data integration
C. Transaction data integration
D. Analytics data integration
E. Fact data integration
questions.correctAnswer: A. Reference data integration

questions.questionText: Initiatives focused on building a 'single view of customer' mainly rely on which DMBOK knowledge area?
options: A. Data Security
B. Data Storage and Operations
C. Reference and Master Data
D. Metadata Management
E. Data Architecture
questions.correctAnswer: C. Reference and Master Data

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a directive that codifies principles and management intent into fundamental rules governing the creation, acquisition, integrity, security, quality, and use of data and information?
options: A. Data Management
B. Data policies
C. Data audit principle
D. Data asset valuation
E. Data Governance
questions.correctAnswer: B. Data policies

questions.questionText: Which of the following is the best example of a 'documented data quality rule'?
options: A. The transaction data from all satellite systems needs to be ready by 12:05 am in order to feed the overnight batching window, to ensure branches have access to actual customer balances
B. All transaction data in the core banking systems need to be processed at 12:05 am each day regardless of the business calendar day and timezone
C. Every transaction recorded must be processed by 12:05 am by authorized personnel who will validate balances and data delivery to branches
D. Each transaction data file holding customer transactions must be kept confidential to the authorized users within the operations team
E. The transaction data from all satellite systems needs to reflect actual customer balances each morning
questions.correctAnswer: A. The transaction data from all satellite systems needs to be ready by 12:05 am in order to feed the overnight batching window, to ensure branches have access to actual customer balances

questions.questionText: Compound authorization groups provide a means to:
options: A. Distract the data security officer
B. Precisely configure an individual's access to a system
C. Effectively prepare for data security audits
D. Obfuscate a user's actual access to a system
E. Encrypt sensitive transmissions of data
questions.correctAnswer: B. Precisely configure an individual's access to a system

questions.questionText: When projects re-use data, the enterprise data architects provide oversight for:
options: A. Adherence to all data standards
B. Integration from original sources
C. Data use for stated purposes
D. Adherence to all project approved end uses
E. Data profiling and remediation
questions.correctAnswer: A. Adherence to all data standards

questions.questionText: The data architect needs to propagate data across the landscape in real time. This requires the leveraging of the following DMBoK knowledge areas:
options: A. Data architecture, data governance and metadata management
B. Data architecture, data quality and content, and document management
C. Data architecture, data modelling and design, and data security
D. Data architecture, metadata management and data security
E. Data architecture, integration and interoperability, data storage and operations
questions.correctAnswer: E. Data architecture, integration and interoperability, data storage and operations

questions.questionText: Evidence management requires document control that includes:
options: A. The ability to store and replicate a source document
B. The ability to maintain the source of sensitive data
C. The ability to prove the source and integrity of a document
D. The ability to store and recover critical data
E. The ability to make any of the documents available
questions.correctAnswer: C. The ability to prove the source and integrity of a document

questions.questionText: A search engine database is populated by a web crawler or spider software that usually processes as:
options: A. Atomic, consistent, isolated, durable (ACID)
B. Continually repeating, always performing (CRAP)
C. Basically available, soft state, eventually consistent (BASE)
D. Fundamentally available, consistently true (FACT)
E. Linearly independent, near exact (LINE)
questions.correctAnswer: C. Basically available, soft state, eventually consistent (BASE)

questions.questionText: DMBoK Knowledge areas should be implemented:
options: A. One at a time to ensure the purity of each solution
B. In a clockwise manner that starts with data quality
C. With at least 2 others to achieve breadth of business activity
D. With each other to achieve coordinated business outcomes
E. With data governance and data quality at the core
questions.correctAnswer: D. With each other to achieve coordinated business outcomes

questions.questionText: Information is created and used by:
options: A. Computers
B. Technology
C. Applications
D. Processes
E. Users
questions.correctAnswer: E. Users

questions.questionText: When reviewing a Data Quality metric approaching its threshold, an experienced Data Quality Manager will:
options: A. Start a review of the metric to determine what the appropriate threshold should be
B. Start working on the issue to avoid the metric going over the threshold
C. Wait for the issue to reach threshold and then lodge an IT incident report
D. Wait for the issue to reach threshold and then initiate a Data Governance response
E. Discuss with your manager ways to take leave over the next month or so
questions.correctAnswer: B. Start working on the issue to avoid the metric going over the threshold

questions.questionText: Which is the most important Data Quality dimension?
options: A. Completeness
B. Consistency
C. Integrity
D. Timeliness
E. It is impossible to identify the most important Data Quality dimension
questions.correctAnswer: E. It is impossible to identify the most important Data Quality dimension

questions.questionText: The main difference between a System of Record and a System of Reference is:
options: A. A system of record is the source of master data; a system of reference is the source of reference data
B. The data does not originate in the system of record
C. The data does not originate in the system of reference
D. They are the same thing
E. A system of reference is the source of transaction data; the system of record is the source of master data
questions.correctAnswer: A. A system of record is the source of master data; a system of reference is the source of reference data

questions.questionText: What differentiates enterprise data architecture from project data architecture?
options: A. Ability to enforce rules
B. A need to control for data quality
C. A good understanding of company policies
D. A collaborative approach to solutions
E. A mandate to look holistically at the enterprise data
questions.correctAnswer: E. A mandate to look holistically at the enterprise data

questions.questionText: What business function is best aligned to deliver oversight to data architecture?
options: A. Business Integration
B. Data Governance
C. Chief Technology Office
D. Business Innovation
E. Enterprise Architecture
questions.correctAnswer: E. Enterprise Architecture

questions.questionText: Data architecture compliance rate measures:
options: A. How closely projects are meeting their timelines
B. How fast the database can retrieve data
C. How closely projects comply with established data architectures
D. How closely projects comply with the development lifecycle
E. How complete an attribute list is in an entity
questions.correctAnswer: C. How closely projects comply with established data architectures

questions.questionText: De-identifying sensitive data in a data warehouse enables:
options: A. A focus on re-identifying records with 3rd party datasets
B. Increased utilization without compromising data privacy
C. A reduced complexity in the data models
D. A bypass in the need to assess data quality
E. Certainty of complete datasets for interrogation
questions.correctAnswer: B. Increased utilization without compromising data privacy

questions.questionText: A catastrophic system failure due to processing attachments that are too large may be solved by:
options: A. Changing the network bandwidth to handle infinitely sized attachments
B. Associating attachment size with the attachment and changing the process to check the size is less than the upper limit
C. Increasing the available memory on the database
D. Associating attachment date with the attachment and changing the process to check the date is less than 1 month old
E. Compressing the attachment before sending it in an email
questions.correctAnswer: B. Associating attachment size with the attachment and changing the process to check the size is less than the upper limit

questions.questionText: How are Critical Data Elements identified?
options: A. By determining which data elements are used by the most processes
B. By examining the most significant regulatory reports
C. By executing the Data vs Process intersection analysis in an integrated Process / Data Models repository
D. By following ISO 8000
E. By linking them to the Data Quality business drivers; customer experience, effectiveness, and efficiency
questions.correctAnswer: C. By executing the Data vs Process intersection analysis in an integrated Process / Data Models repository

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a stage in the Shewhart / Deming Cycle that drives the data quality improvement lifecycle?
options: A. Do
B. Correct
C. Investigate
D. Rerun
E. Perform
questions.correctAnswer: A. Do

questions.questionText: A business driver for the creation of a Data Quality Management program is:
options: A. Improve time-to-market by reducing system development lifecycle time
B. To increase the probability of industry awards
C. To ensure the data quality team maintains maximum efficiency
D. To reduce risks and costs associated with poor data quality
E. To assist in the consolidation of data warehouses and data lakes
questions.correctAnswer: D. To reduce risks and costs associated with poor data quality

questions.questionText: A 'Data Swamp' is a data lake that has become:
options: A. A data asset that uses machine learning
B. Modeled, managed and muddy
C. Overly cataloged, holding information and data
D. Messy, unclean and inconsistent
E. Suitable for frogs, toads and salamanders
questions.correctAnswer: D. Messy, unclean and inconsistent

questions.questionText: Data mining is:
options: A. Advanced digging into the data to determine the likely causes of data quality failures
B. Picking through the data in the data warehouse looking for 'gems'
C. The classification of raw data as ore which is subsequently subjected to a process of refinement
D. Analysis using 'unsupervised' machine learning techniques without knowledge of the desired outcome
E. The scoping up of data through a rotating artificial intelligence 'scraping' mechanism
questions.correctAnswer: B. Picking through the data in the data warehouse looking for 'gems'

questions.questionText: A project scope requires the collection, exchange, and reporting of data from multiple in-house custom systems.Documents gathered include business concepts, existing database schemas, XSD's, and reporting layouts. How many models of each layer of abstraction can be expected?
options: A. More XSD's than reporting layouts.
B. More conceptual data models than physical data models.
C. Only 1 conceptual data model, 1 logical data model and 1 physical data model
D. The same number of each of the model types.
E. More physical data models than logical data models, and more logical data models than conceptual data models.
questions.correctAnswer: E. More physical data models than logical data models, and more logical data models than conceptual data models.

questions.questionText: The purpose for adding redundancy to a data model (denormalisation) is to:
options: A. Make it easier for developers to join tables
B. Fully utilize all the indexes
C. Avoid the loss of data by storing key values more than once
D. Ensure surrogate keys are retaining their unique values in all satellite tables
E. Improve aggregate database performance across read requests
questions.correctAnswer: E. Improve aggregate database performance across read requests

questions.questionText: Different types of metadata include:
options: A. Business, Information, Application and Technology
B. Business, Quality, and Introspective
C. Business, Application and Processing
D. Fluid, static and viscous
E. Business, Technical and Operational
questions.correctAnswer: A. Business, Information, Application and Technology

questions.questionText: Metadata is often categorized into 3 types, they are:
options: A. Technical, infrastructure and instance
B. Business, strategic and meta-metadata
C. Operational, reporting and analytical
D. Business, technical and strategic
E. Business, technical and operational
questions.correctAnswer: E. Business, technical and operational

questions.questionText: The ISO Metadata Registry Standard that provides a framework for defining a metadata registry is:
options: A. ISO 9001
B. ISO / IEC 11179
C. ISO MD 1
E. ISO 4590
questions.correctAnswer: B. ISO / IEC 11179

questions.questionText: Is it better to specify data recoverability requirements or data backup requirements? Why?
options: A. Data recoverability requirements. Data backup is one technique to meet data recoverability requirements
B. Data recoverability requirements. Data backup is not related to business recovery at all
C. Data backup requirements. This will ensure that the data is backed up on a cycle required by the business
D. Data backup requirements. This will enable the IT team to be confident of the business requirement
E. Neither. Data recovery and backup is often avoided altogether using a cloud solution
questions.correctAnswer: A. Data recoverability requirements. Data backup is one technique to meet data recoverability requirements

questions.questionText: Production DBA's would not normally take responsibility for:
options: A. Implementing backup and recovery mechanisms
B. Implementing mechanisms for clustering and failover of the database
C. The fitness of purpose for data in the database
D. Ensuring performance and reliability of the database
E. Executing other database maintenance activities, such as archiving
questions.correctAnswer: C. The fitness of purpose for data in the database

questions.questionText: The acronym ACID stands for:
options: A. Available, corrupt, isolation and durable
B. Atomicity, consistency, isolation and durability
C. Atomicity, completeness, independence and durability
D. Actual, created, identified and deleted
E. Available, completeness, isolation and dangerous
questions.correctAnswer: B. Atomicity, consistency, isolation and durability

questions.questionText: Information security begins by classifying an organisation's data in order to:
options: A. Identify which departments need more data
B. Identify which data needs protection
C. Identify which systems need better reporting
D. Identify which subject area the data belongs in
E. Identify the metadata classification values
questions.correctAnswer: B. Identify which data needs protection

questions.questionText: In data security, which of the following is not one of the four A's:
options: A. Agile
B. Authentication
C. Authorization
D. Access
E. Audit
questions.correctAnswer: A. Agile

questions.questionText: What are the Belmont Principles?
options: A. Respect for Persons, Beneficence, Justice
B. Right to Privacy, Right to Data Access, Right to be Forgotten
C. Data Quality, Data Adequacy, Data Management
D. Human Dignity, Civil Rights, Respect for Privacy
E. Justice, Fairness, Equality
questions.correctAnswer: A. Respect for Persons, Beneficence, Justice

questions.questionText: A diagram containing the existing data stores and data interfaces between them is called a:
options: A. Data landscape
B. Metadata repository
C. Data flow diagram
D. Unsecure data network
E. Dimensional model
questions.correctAnswer: A. Data landscape

questions.questionText: Who does the DMBoK consider to be generally responsible for developing business glossary content?
options: A. Business Users
B. Conceptual Data Modellers
C. Business Data Stewards
D. Coordinating Data Stewards
E. Data Architects
questions.correctAnswer: C. Business Data Stewards

questions.questionText: A weakness or defect in a system that allows it to be successfully attacked and compromised is a:
options: A. Risk
B. Chasm
C. Threat
D. Feature
E. Vulnerability
questions.correctAnswer: E. Vulnerability

questions.questionText: The standard for a strong password is set by the:
options: A. Data security standard
B. Identity management standard
C. Data modelling standard
D. Data naming standard
E. Data access standard
questions.correctAnswer: B. Identity management standard

questions.questionText: The acronym ETL most commonly stands for:
options: A. Extend Trim Load
B. Efficient Trace Logging
C. Extract Transform Load
D. Extract Transpose Leverage
E. Export Transform Log
questions.correctAnswer: C. Extract Transform Load

questions.questionText: The need to manage data movement efficiently is a primary driver for:
options: A. Data Integration and Interoperability
B. Data Security
C. Document and Content Management
D. Data Warehousing and Business Intelligence
E. Data Storage and Operations
questions.correctAnswer: A. Data Integration and Interoperability

questions.questionText: Periodic archiving of transaction data from a production CRM system is critical for:
options: A. Training junior DBAs
B. The maintenance of database performance
C. Enabling the distribution of transaction data across the enterprise
D. Providing alternate sources for reporting systems
E. Managing deleted customer records
questions.correctAnswer: B. The maintenance of database performance

questions.questionText: One of the difficulties when integrating multiple source systems is:
options: A. Modifying the source systems to align to the enterprise data model
B. Completing the data architecture on time for the first release
C. Having a data quality rule applicable to all source systems
D. Determining valid links or equivalences between data elements
E. Maintaining documentation describing the data warehouse operation
questions.correctAnswer: D. Determining valid links or equivalences between data elements

questions.questionText: Data Staging areas are populated from source databases using extract functions, transformed and then:
options: A. Made ready for review by a data modeller, for physical modelling
B. Made ready for metadata repository loading
C. Made ready for metrics calculations and key performance indicators
D. Made ready with advanced profiling and reported into a visualisation tool.
E. Made ready and loaded into the data warehouse
questions.correctAnswer: E. Made ready and loaded into the data warehouse

questions.questionText: When assessing tools to implement master data management solutions, functionality must include:
options: A. Document and content management
B. Auto-normalization features
C. Advanced analytics capabilities
D. Backup and recovery utilities
E. Sophisticated integration capability
questions.correctAnswer: E. Sophisticated integration capability

questions.questionText: Disclosure (e.g. privacy agreements) is the most common way to manage ethical positions. What is one technique to go beyond disclosure?
options: A. Working with government to organise protective legislation
B. Work with stakeholders to test how they react to proposed experiences
C. Talking to other organisations that have had poor ethical behaviours exposed
D. Working only with internal people to minimise cost and complexity
E. Working with the media to organise better press releases
questions.correctAnswer: B. Work with stakeholders to test how they react to proposed experiences

questions.questionText: The purpose and rationale for data integration should be defined by:
options: A. Reporting requirements
B. The developers
C. Industry standards
D. Interoperability requirements
E. The business
questions.correctAnswer: E. The business

questions.questionText: A denial of service attack is typically accomplished by:
options: A. Flooding the target machine with superfluous requests
B. Interrupting the mains electricity supply
C. A stop-work action by the workforce
D. Corrupting the user name and passwords
E. Emailing virus laden attachments
questions.correctAnswer: A. Flooding the target machine with superfluous requests

questions.questionText: Which of the following activities is most likely to maintain bias in data analysis?
options: A. Re-baselining the data set after each period of analysis to remove assumptions
B. Remove sources of likely bias from data sets before analysis
C. Using more data sets with diverse data sources
D. Working with external stakeholders to assess data quality
E. Using more data sets with same data
questions.correctAnswer: E. Using more data sets with same data

questions.questionText: What causes data redundancy or data ROT?
options: A. Poor assimilation of collected data
B. Poor data management practices
C. Server and human error
D. Dataset inaccuracies developed over time
E. All of these
questions.correctAnswer: E. All of these

questions.questionText: A Data Quality dimension is:
options: A. The value of a particular piece of data
B. A valid value in a list
C. A core concept in dimensional modeling
D. A measurable feature or characteristic of data
E. One aspect of data quality used extensively in data governance
questions.correctAnswer: D. A measurable feature or characteristic of data

questions.questionText: The Data Governance Organisation and Enterprise Data Architecture closely align because:
options: A. Enterprise data architecture must align to data governance standards and procedures
B. Often, data architects act as business liaisons for data governance activities
C. It is not necessary
D. Standard and procedure development requires close collaboration
E. Data architects act as Data Governance liaisons to IT
questions.correctAnswer: A. Enterprise data architecture must align to data governance standards and procedures

questions.questionText: The necessity of representing organisational data at different levels of abstraction is:
options: A. Because most database administrators need specifications to build databases with appropriate response times
B. Because most architects want to deploy a complete suite of drawings for project deliverables
C. Because most organisations need to accommodate to the different points of view of information architecture and data architecture
D. Because most Chief Data Officers do not have the technical background to be held accountable for complex data diagrams
E. Because most organisations have more data than individual people can comprehend, understand and make decisions about
questions.correctAnswer: C. Because most organisations need to accommodate to the different points of view of information architecture and data architecture

questions.questionText: How is the Agile development method dependent on the data-architect?
options: A. Short sprints can be reviewed after completion by reversing engineering the data
B. Compliance with well-defined standards & guidelines
C. Development Sprints will be pre-emptied by data model delivery package
D. Object-oriented designs can be validated by the enterprise data models
questions.correctAnswer: C. Development Sprints will be pre-emptied by data model delivery package

questions.questionText: What is the most common label to describe responsibility for ensuring effective control and use of data assets?
options: A. Business Drivership
B. Data Operations
C. Data Custodianship
D. Data Stewardship
E. Data Quality Custodianship
questions.correctAnswer: D. Data Stewardship

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of Data Governance?
options: A. Encompass the entire lifecycle of a data asset
B. To ensure that data is managed properly, according to policies and best practices
C. Ensure an organization gets value out of its data
D. Establish processes and functions through which data can be enabled for use and also maintained.
E. Ensure that financial performance of the company is improved
questions.correctAnswer: B. To ensure that data is managed properly, according to policies and best practices

questions.questionText: A schema is:
options: A. A logical data model
B. A business conceptual data model
C. A database object that increases performance of an application
D. A subset of the overall database for security and/or operational purposes
E. A plot to achieve better data governance
questions.correctAnswer: D. A subset of the overall database for security and/or operational purposes

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of ISO 8000?
options: A. Create, collect, store, maintain, transfer, process, and present metadata
B. Promote timely and cost-effective data integration
C. Enable the exchange of complex information in an application-neutral form
D. Report on potential data security risk
questions.correctAnswer: C. Enable the exchange of complex information in an application-neutral form

questions.questionText: During planning stage of a data model which of the following is not a valid scheme?
options: A. NoSQL
B. Fact-based
C. Dimensional
D. Denormalised
E. Relational
questions.correctAnswer: A. NoSQL

questions.questionText: Which artifact is the highest level of abstraction in the Enterprise Data Model?
options: A. Data Ownership Model
B. Subject Area model
C. Conceptual Model
D. Top-level Process Model
E. Systems Portfolio Model
questions.correctAnswer: C. Conceptual Model

questions.questionText: Which statement best describes the relationship between documents and records?
options: A. Records are a sub-set of documents
B. Documents and records are not related
C. Documents are written and records are audio
D. Documents are a sub-set of records
E. Documents and records are the same thing
questions.correctAnswer: A. Records are a sub-set of documents

questions.questionText: An umbrella term for any classification or controlled vocabulary is:
options: A. Taxonomy
B. English
C. Metadata
D. Dictionary
E. Data model
questions.correctAnswer: A. Taxonomy

questions.questionText: Business continuity is an aspect of Governance. What should a business continuity plan include?
options: A. Outlines how a business will continue operating during an unplanned disruption in service
B. Provides explanation to customers during an unplanned disruption in service
C. Defines unplanned disruptions that may occur
D. Precedes business rules
E. Explains to external stakeholders why performance expectations are not being met
questions.correctAnswer: A. Outlines how a business will continue operating during an unplanned disruption in service

questions.questionText: Considerations for whether to integrate two data stores should include all except the:
options: A. Ability to govern the data across both systems
B. Capability of the systems to support the data natively
C. Complexity of data operations
D. Privacy and access consideration for the data
E. Maintaining of master data trust across the two data stores
questions.correctAnswer: C. Complexity of data operations

questions.questionText: Misclassification of transactions through integration is often caused by inconsistent:
options: A. Data quality
B. Master data
C. Content management
D. Business intelligence
E. Reference data
questions.correctAnswer: E. Reference data

questions.questionText: A database that is growing at 100% per annum compound will be:
options: A. 3 times its original size at the end of year 2
B. Half its original size at the end of year 2
C. 2 times its original size at the end of year 5
D. 4 times its original size at the end of year 2
E. 6 times its original size at the end of year
questions.correctAnswer: D. 4 times its original size at the end of year 2

questions.questionText: A machine, learning algorithm that incorrectly classifies new data values, may have a problem with population imbalances in:
options: A. Metadata management
B. Reference data management
C. Model training data
D. Predictive analytics
E. Big data architecture
questions.correctAnswer: C. Model training data

questions.questionText: If two data stores are able to be inconsistent during normal operations, then the integration approach is:
options: A. Faulty
B. Uncontrolled
C. Streaming
D. Asynchronous
E. Synchronous
questions.correctAnswer: D. Asynchronous

questions.questionText: What position is responsible for the quality and use of their organization's data assets?
options: A. Chief Information Officer
B. Data Scientist
C. Data Modeler
D. Data Architect
E. Data Steward
questions.correctAnswer: E. Data Steward

questions.questionText: What is the definition of a business rule in the context of Data Governance?
options: A. Outlines the steps to take when a business disruption occurs
B. Clarifies an organization's goal

D. Specifies constraints on what can and cannot be done in the organization
E. Contains an organization's contingency plans
questions.correctAnswer: D. Specifies constraints on what can and cannot be done in the organization

questions.questionText: Information governance and data governance should be:

C. Managed by the Chief Information Officer
D. Managed as a single function
E. Managed as integrated functions, with data governance reporting to information governance
questions.correctAnswer: E. Managed as integrated functions, with data governance reporting to information governance

questions.questionText: A â€˜Data Lakeâ€™ is an environment where a vast amount of data can be:

E. Ingested, screened, obfuscated and purged
questions.correctAnswer: D. Ingested, shared, assessed and analysed

questions.questionText: The right to be forgotten is:
options: A. A person's right to have imperfect memory

D. A person's right to have their information removed from all databases on earth

questions.correctAnswer: B. A personâ€™s right to have all their information erased from an organisation

questions.questionText: What Data Architecture artifacts provide the Data Modelling Project boundaries?
options: A. Enterprise Data Model

questions.correctAnswer: C. Business Data Model

questions.questionText: Which has the highest value return for an enterprise data architect?
options: A. Influencing the solution options for projects during initiation

C. Reviewing operational business continuity plans
D. Assisting a project team design a solution
E. Guiding the testing and data migration
questions.correctAnswer: A. Influencing the solution options for projects during initiation

questions.questionText: The stakeholder requirements for privacy and confidentiality are goals found in:

B. Data quality
C. Data security
D. Document and content management
E. Metadata management
questions.correctAnswer: C. Data security

questions.questionText: When measuring the value of data architecture one should be most concerned about:
options: A. Cost savings
B. Improved coding efficiency
C. Added business value
D. Number of Projects reviewed
E. Project speed
questions.correctAnswer: C. Added business value

questions.questionText: Typical types of assessments used to describe an organisationâ€™s state of information management capability, do not include:
options: A. Capacity to Change

C. Data Management Maturity
D. Business Alignment
E. Security and Penetration Testing
questions.correctAnswer: E. Security and Penetration Testing

questions.questionText: What is Data Stewardship?
options: A. Refers to the role responsible for creating policies, procedures, and rules that govern data in the organization
B. A collection of tools that ensure an organization's privacy policy
C. A prioritized program of work with scoped boundaries
D. A position accountable and responsible for data and processes that ensure effective control and use of data assets
E. The creation of compelling vision for Data Management across the enterprise
questions.correctAnswer: D. A position accountable and responsible for data and processes that ensure effective control and use of data assets

questions.questionText: What position should be responsible for leading the Data Governance Council (DGC)?
options: A. Chief Data Architect or Chief Data Modeler
B. Chief Data Steward or Chief Data Officer

D. DGC Chair should rotate among executive positions
E. Any executive can chair the DGC
questions.correctAnswer: B. Chief Data Steward or Chief Data Officer

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of data lineage?
options: A. Formal Data Quality assessment of a dataset

D. List of potential data integration opportunities
E. Collection of all the metadata related to a dataset
questions.correctAnswer: B. Tracking of historical changes to a dataset

questions.questionText: Reference data is often a list of code values with their full names. One example is:

E. Person age associated with accessibility requirements

questions.questionText: According to the DMBoK, which of these is not a commonly used entity category:
options: A. Who

C. If
D. What
E. Why
questions.correctAnswer: C. If

E. The capture and maintenance of source structures for each attribute on the data model
questions.correctAnswer: E. The capture and maintenance of source structures for each attribute on the data model

questions.questionText: The purpose of Data Quality Management is to create data that is:

C. Accurate and precise
D. Backed up and recoverable
E. Reliable and trustworthy
questions.correctAnswer: E. Reliable and trustworthy

questions.questionText: Critical Data is most often used in:
options: A. Regulatory, financial, or management reporting

questions.correctAnswer: A. Regulatory, financial, or management reporting

questions.questionText: The repeated implementation of different CRM technologies with different data structures is mostly a failure of:
options: A. Data security

questions.questionText: A data model:

D. Must exist in a data modelling tool

questions.questionText: The business case for enterprise warehousing is:
options: A. To align data systems, improve source system management, and to enable an enterprise exploit machine learning techniques

D. To increase data redundancy, align information glossaries, and enable an enterprise to exploit machine learning techniques
E. To reduce data redundancy, improve information consistency, and enable an enterprise to use its data to make better decisions
questions.correctAnswer: E. To reduce data redundancy, improve information consistency, and enable an enterprise to use its data to make better decisions

questions.questionText: One of the principles for the implementation of a data warehouse is:
options: A. Think and design globally; act and build locally

D. Focus on project goals over business goals; design internally and outsource build

questions.questionText: An annual enterprise data architecture report is comprised of a summary of enterprise data model updates, project compliance and variances, and a measurement of:

questions.questionText: 'Top down' and 'bottom up' data analysis and profiling is best done in concert because:

questions.questionText: 'Open by default' document control will assist data sharing by:

questions.correctAnswer: B. Can be considered as a foundational blueprint for data for the organisation, and Data Architecture blueprints are one of the key deliverables for Data Architecture

questions.questionText: How is Data Governance defined?

E. Data governance assists in representing information consistently and protecting sensitive information
questions.correctAnswer: A. Exercise of the authority and control over the management of data assets.

questions.questionText: Common understanding of the core business concepts and terminology is the objective of which deliverable?

questions.questionText: Master data is data about:

questions.questionText: Critical to the incremental development of the data warehouse is:

questions.questionText: In data modelling, it is important to know if time is being modelled because:
